rewrites.rewritten_text: Led the design and technical architecture of a retrofittable drive-by-wire actuation system for an autonomous golf cart, integrating electronic steering, throttle, and braking through three linear actuators and embedded control interfaces.

Authored Interface Control Documents (ICDs) including hardware-software interfaces, 30+ I/O signals, actuator command structures, GPIO mappings, and subsystem communication architecture supporting system-level embedded development and integration.

Designed and deployed sensor architecture and wiring harnesses including RTK GPS, IMU, ultrasonic, LiDAR, and camera arrays, achieving 360-degree environmental perception with under 2 cm localization error.
Collaborated on YOLOv8 and OpenCV perception with over 90 percent accuracy at 30 FPS.

Built a Python-based ETL platform to extract, validate, and normalize sales data from three major distributor systems into a unified report, processing thousands of transaction records across multiple reporting formats.

Engineered repeatable Pandas and NumPy pipelines to standardize SKU, promotion, and sales data, reducing manual reporting effort by approximately 40 to 50 percent and improving cross-unit data consistency.

Modeled custom actuator assemblies in SolidWorks with integrated harmonic drives, specifying torque-speed profiles, encoder resolution, and gear ratios for positioning accuracy within 0.1 degrees and minimal backlash.
Developed supervisory safety logic and emergency-stop control strategies, ensuring robust functional safety across every deployment.
